Latest browser options
Measure potential to learn, adapt, and perform in any role with SHL Verify Interactive cognitive assessments.

Our pre-employment cognitive assessment starts by asking candidates a series of interactive questions organized around a central principle linked to something they are likely to encounter in the role.
Measures general mental ability, or G, and effectively measures candidates’ problem-solving and critical reasoning capabilities.

Measures candidates’ ability to draw logical conclusions and complete scenarios based on the information provided. Tasks include reading statements, then determining which subsequent options cannot be true or must be true.

Measures candidates’ ability to draw inferences from, and understand relationships between, various concepts. Tasks include working out the pattern in a set of images and identifying the correct next image.

Measures candidates’ ability to work with numbers and use appropriate mathematics in different situations. Tasks include solving word problems, performing numerical calculations, and interpreting charts and graphs.

SHL's Verify Interactive delivers precise scores indicating candidates' cognitive ability. These scores, along with other SHL talent acquisition data such as behavior and personality assessments, culminate in a well-rounded job score for each candidate. SHL's cognitive assessments aim to empower your teams to make fair and efficient talent choices, leading to business success.

headings: A Smarter Way to Assess Spoken Language
What This Means for Your Business
paragraphs: We recommend upgrading to a modern browser.
If you choose to continue with your current browser we cannot guarantee your experience.
Latest browser options
Assess genuine language skills across speaking, vocabulary, grammar, listening and comprehension — giving you a reliable signal you can trust in high-volume hiring.
SHL SVAR is our automated spoken-language assessment designed to measure real communication ability.
It evaluates how candidates speak and converse to give you a clear, evidence‑based view of workplace‑ready communication.

SVAR supports 20+ languages and 7 accents, works on mobile and web, and takes just 12–15 minutes to complete. AI‑powered scoring delivers fast, consistent results so hiring teams can assess spoken‑language capability at scale.

SHL SVAR measures the essentials of spoken communication in one quick assessment; how clearly candidates speak, their pronunciation and fluency, how well they understand and respond to information, and how accurately they use vocabulary and grammar.
And because it captures spontaneous, real-time speech, you get a genuine view of a candidate's spoken ability rather than a rehearsed response.
Automated scoring delivers fast, consistent results, helping you screen high-volume and multilingual talent pools with confidence.
It’s designed to be simple for candidates and efficient for hiring teams.
The result: a fast, transparent and evidence‑based view of spoken language capability.
SHL SVAR combines globally recognized language standards with AI-driven speech analysis to deliver reliable, scalable spoken language assessment.
Clear communication leads to better experiences and stronger relationships
Shared language standards support smoother internal and external communication
Accurate assessment means hiring candidates with the right language skills from day one
Consistent language standards across international operations
SHL SVAR is built for roles where spoken communication directly impacts performance, customer experience, and service quality.

SHL SVAR helps teams quickly identify candidates who can communicate clearly, confidently, and effectively from day one.

Fill your talent pools with the skills of the future to ensure your workforce can flex as their environments change. Find candidates at scale that will quickly make an impact and grow with your organization.

Accurately assess critical skills such as customer service, communication and language capabilities to identify candidates with the right skills and fit for BPO roles – while managing high volumes and controlling costs.

Customer care roles can be demanding and it takes a specific skillset to succeed. Identify candidates with the skills to deliver excellent customer service and adapt in a fast-changing contact center environment.

Measure potential to learn, adapt, and perform in any role with SHL Verify Interactive cognitive assessments.

Our pre-employment cognitive assessment starts by asking candidates a series of interactive questions organized around a central principle linked to something they are likely to encounter in the role.
Measures general mental ability, or G, and effectively measures candidates’ problem-solving and critical reasoning capabilities.

Measures candidates’ ability to draw logical conclusions and complete scenarios based on the information provided. Tasks include reading statements, then determining which subsequent options cannot be true or must be true.

Measures candidates’ ability to draw inferences from, and understand relationships between, various concepts. Tasks include working out the pattern in a set of images and identifying the correct next image.

Measures candidates’ ability to work with numbers and use appropriate mathematics in different situations. Tasks include solving word problems, performing numerical calculations, and interpreting charts and graphs.

SHL's Verify Interactive delivers precise scores indicating candidates' cognitive ability. These scores, along with other SHL talent acquisition data such as behavior and personality assessments, culminate in a well-rounded job score for each candidate. SHL's cognitive assessments aim to empower your teams to make fair and efficient talent choices, leading to business success.

headings: A Clear Picture of Workplace Skills Across People and Teams
Bring Objective Skills Insight into Every Talent Decision
Inside the SHL Global Skills Assessment
What Makes the SHL Global Skills Assessment Different?
See AI Readiness in the Skills You Already Measure
paragraphs: We recommend upgrading to a modern browser.
If you choose to continue with your current browser we cannot guarantee your experience.
Latest browser options
One Assessment, Every Talent Decision
96 behavioral skills measured in 15 minutes. Objective, consistent skills data you can use from hiring decisions to development conversations to internal mobility.

The SHL Global Skills Assessment gives organizations a consistent, science‑based view of behavioral skills across candidates and employees. It provides a shared language for understanding strengths, gaps, and the behaviors that drive performance.

Because it’s built on one universal framework, the assessment creates a unified picture of skills across your organization. You can compare people fairly, understand capability at scale, and build a stronger foundation for development, mobility, and long‑term workforce planning.
With one consistent framework before and after hire, you can use the SHL Global Skills Assessment in hiring, across your workforce, or both.
Include the SHL Global Skills Assessment in role-specific hiring to identify your top candidates.
Reuse their skills profiles at onboarding to guide targeted development from day one.
Give the SHL Global Skills Assessment directly to employees.
In 15 minutes per person, you gain organization-wide visibility into capabilities through detailed reporting.
The assessment measures all 96 skills in SHL’s Universal Competency Framework, giving you a complete view of the workplace behaviors that drive performance across roles.

In around 15 minutes, individuals compare short behavioral statements and select the ones most like them. This simple, forced‑choice format captures authentic workplace behavior while reducing response bias.

The assessment produces a range of reports tailored to your use case. Individuals receive development insights they can use for onboarding or growth. HR teams can view results for single candidates or aggregated groups, and—when used for selection—generate structured interview guides or role‑aligned talent profiles.

Alongside current strengths and gaps, the Reskilling Potential add‑on shows who has the willingness and ability to successfully upskill. This gives you a clearer view of who can grow into future roles, projects, and opportunities.
Normed with global samples representative across job levels and geographies to enable consistent comparisons
Built to support fair practices grounded in EEOC guidance for equal opportunity
Make decisions you can defend because you're using validated methods with documented job relevance

The Global Skills Assessment gives you a complete picture of your workforce’s behavioral skills. Our SHL AI Readiness Profile applies a focused lens to that data, revealing who’s ready to work effectively in an AI-powered environment and where to build capability next.
Learn more about AI Readiness
SHL Global Skills Assessment

Most organizations have multiple frameworks—one for hiring, another for performance, a third for leadership. The UCF gives you a single, validated framework that works across all of them. You can map your existing frameworks to the UCF (we help with this), which means you finally get consistent, comparable data. Plus, the UCF’s Skills Taxonomy is normed globally and mapped to 600+ O*NET profiles and 55,000 job titles, something custom frameworks can't match.

The GSA reveals individual strengths and skills gaps, making it clear where development is needed. It also identifies employees with high adaptability and ability to learn, those most ready to reskill into new roles as business needs evolve. This turns skills data into targeted development actions at individual, team, and organizational levels.

Skills data collected during hiring remain accessible throughout the employee lifecycle. The same objective profile that informed the hiring decision becomes the foundation for onboarding conversations, development planning, and internal movement—no repeat assessment required.
Yes. 15+ independent validation studies across industries show GSA composites consistently predict supervisor-rated performance and productivity.
Global norms allow comparison across job levels, roles, and geographies using the same benchmark—whether you're assessing 50 people in one location or 50,000 across a global workforce.
Make smarter hiring decisions with skills data that you can carry forward through the employee lifecycle.
Uncover skills gaps and build targeted development plans that align to business objectives.
Use objective skills data to match employees to the right internal opportunities, and vice versa.
SHL and its affiliates. All rights reserved.

Job-Focused Assessments

SHL’s Job-Focused Assessments predict job success by evaluating key hard and soft skills tailored to specific roles. Applicants take a short, engaging assessment relevant to the job and you gain a comprehensive view of candidates' role readiness, team fit and future potential.
Fast and accurate measurement of job-relevant skills using patented Apta technology.

Get a well-rounded view of each candidate’s level of potential, readiness, and fit through one single assessment. Job-Focused Assessments utilize Apta, a direct measure of observable competency behaviors linked to performance, which has been proven to reduce faking and impression management.
Attract best-fit talent with skills assessments that are directly related to the role they are applying for.
Measure the skills and competencies that matter most to any specific job or role level. Pre-packaged assessments to ensure quick deployment.
Select candidates based on the skills they possess or ability and desire to grow, without relying on past experience and formal qualifications.

Unlock the full potential of your hiring process through SHL’s hiring solution packages, which all include job-focused assessments. Evaluate the essential skills for any role and beyond, while providing candidates with a memorable and intuitive experience.
Identify candidates who excel in delivering exceptional customer service and issue resolution.
Spot candidates who meet high customer expectations and thrive in uncertain environments.
Find top talent for roles that demand high-quality, safe, and reliable work.
Select candidates with the ability to learn quickly to build a future-ready talent pipeline.
Evaluate today’s critical and measure reskilling potential across various individual contributor roles.
Identify managerial skills, potential, and reskilling ability to support the ever-changing demands of people management.
Companies using SHL’s Job-Focused Assessments report significant improvements.
40% less likely to hire tardy production workers
40% more likely to acquire new customers through contact center agents
73% more capable of handling difficult customers through service reps
66% more likely to achieve top sales performance ratings
Objectively measure the right skills across your recruitment process, whatever the role.
